name: Secure Token Generator
creator: Python core developers
breakability: Tokens are cryptographically unpredictable. Not applicable.
modern_use: API keys, session tokens, password reset links, CSRF tokens, temporary codes
description: The Python secrets module generates cryptographically strong random numbers suitable for managing tokens, such as account authentication, security tokens, and similar secrets. It uses the OS-level CSPRNG (urandom on Unix, BCryptGenRandom on Windows).
notes: Use secrets.compare_digest() when comparing tokens to prevent timing attacks. Never use random module for security purposes.
did_you_know: Python's random module is NOT cryptographically secure. Its output is predictable if you know the seed. The secrets module was added specifically to discourage misuse of random for security.
